---
title: "Weather_Graphs"
author: "Wacker"
date: "2026-04-14"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

##Some graphs on the data I made while studying for the exam

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ZaileyFlagWeather)
data("Flagstaff_Weather")
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ggplot(Flagstaff_Weather, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = tmax), color = "red", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_line(aes(y = tmin), color = "blue", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = tmax), color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = tmin), color = "blue", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Daily Maximum and Minimum Temperature",
       subtitle = "Flagstaff Pulliam Airport (1950–2019)",
       x = "Date", y = "Temperature (°F)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
annual_tmax <- Flagstaff_Weather |>
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(date)) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(avg_tmax = mean(tmax, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(annual_tmax, aes(x = year, y = avg_tmax)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkred") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Annual Average Maximum Temperature",
       x = "Year", 
       y = "Average TMAX (°F)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
annual_prcp <- Flagstaff_Weather |>
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(date)) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(total_prcp = sum(prcp, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(annual_prcp, aes(x = year, y = total_prcp)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Annual Total Precipitation",
       x = "Year", 
       y = "Total Precipitation (inches)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
annual_snow <- Flagstaff_Weather |>
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(date)) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(total_snow = sum(snow, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(annual_snow, aes(x = year, y = total_snow)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkblue") +
  labs(title = "Annual Total Snowfall",
       x = "Year", 
       y = "Total Snowfall (inches)") +
  theme_minimal()
```
